title: Klasea mouterdei
intro: Klasea mouterdei, also known as Mouterde's klasea, is a species of plant in the Boraginaceae family, commonly found in the Mediterranean region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which have been linked to potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Klasea mouterdei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made with Klasea mouterdei include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of various inflammatory conditions.

This plant is further classified within the genus Klasea, which is a small group of flowering plants. The species Klasea mouterdei is native to certain regions and is a member of the order Lamiales, a large and diverse group of flowering plants.
